title: Touchpoint
description: A junction where two or more things interact. Wherever more than two things meet, the touchpoint records HOW they connect: the surface exposed and the protocol spoken (plus payload format, direction, and endpoint). Every touchpoint MUST declare a surface and a protocol — if no protocol exists yet, the touchpoint is the place where one is defined.

surface.description: Interface kind exposed: api | ui | cli | event | file | database | webhook | stream | agent | tool.

protocol.description: How parties communicate: http | https | grpc | graphql | websocket | sql | amqp | mqtt | kafka | s3 | smtp | a2a | mcp | custom. Note: a2a (Agent-to-Agent) is agent↔agent — same type on both sides, so it is the SEAMLESS case and not itself a boundary touchpoint. The boundary is where an agent meets a different type: agent↔tool uses mcp (Model Context Protocol). Required — if no protocol exists for a boundary, define one.

format.description: Payload format: json | jsonld | protobuf | avro | csv | form | binary.

protocolVersion.description: Protocol/contract version, e.g. HTTP/2, gRPC v1, OpenAPI 3.1.
